response.justification: The candidate is an accomplished full-stack and web software developer with over two decades of experience, but his background is fundamentally misaligned with this role. The position requires SoC and IP modeling, AI accelerator performance analysis, and hardware-software co-design at a principal level, while his career centers on web platforms, product engineering, and developer tooling in TypeScript, Node.js, and DLang. Brief low-latency systems work and C/C++ experience do not substitute for hardware architecture, GPU, or deep-learning workload optimization expertise, none of which appear on the resume.
strengths: 20+ years of broad software engineering experience with a Bachelor's in Computer Science, satisfying the formal experience bar
Low-latency systems experience at Sociomantic (async I/O, fibers, caching, high-throughput microservices in DLang)
Some C/C++ background from National Instruments plus recent exposure to AI tooling (MCP, agentic workflows)
concerns: No SoC or IP modeling, silicon architecture, or hardware-software co-design experience anywhere in his history
No GPU architecture, SIMD/MIMD, or deep neural network training/inference optimization background
Career trajectory is web/full-stack product engineering, far from principal-level hardware performance architecture

key_factors.factor: No SoC/IP modeling or hardware architecture experience
No AI accelerator, GPU, or deep-learning workload optimization background
Low-latency systems programming and long engineering track record
